Comment: Coaxial helicopter on a RealFlight coaxial heli model. DESIGN RULE: this table is a PURE IDENTITY PIPE for all twelve channels - rf N == px4 N, RealFlight channel N+1 is driven by PX4 output channel N+1, with no renumbering and no reversal anywhere. The ordering decision lives entirely in PWM_MAIN_FUNC* (SITL, ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d-posix/airframes/1217_flightaxis_helicoax) and HIL_ACT_FUNC* (HITL, ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d/airframes/1217_flightaxis_helicoax.hil), the same way ArduPilot uses SERVOn_FUNCTION and PX4's own gz bridge uses SIM_GZ_*_FUNC*. Do not re-scramble the map here; change the FUNC params instead. Two numberings must be kept apart. (1) The ALLOCATOR's internal numbering, which is a property of CA_AIRFRAME and is UNCHANGED by any of this: CA_AIRFRAME 12 (Coaxial) adds actuators motors-first, then servos, so it is always Motor1=101 clockwise main rotor [0,1], Motor2=102 counter-clockwise main rotor [0,1], Servo1..Servo3=201..203 swash plate servos 1-3 [-1,1]. Unlike the single-rotor frame there is NO tail rotor: yaw is DIFFERENTIAL rotor throttle and collective is COMMON rotor throttle (ActuatorEffectivenessHelicopterCoaxial.cpp: rotor CW = throttle - yaw, rotor CCW = throttle + yaw), and the swash carries only cyclic (roll/pitch). Because no tail consumes Servo1, the swash starts at Servo1=201 here, not 202 as on the single-rotor frame. (2) The controls[] POSITIONS, freely chosen by PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N>/HIL_ACT_FUNC<N> (function N lands on controls[N-1]): controls[0..2]=swash plate servos 1-3 (functions 201/202/203), controls[6]=clockwise rotor (function 101), controls[7]=counter-clockwise rotor (function 102). NEEDS TUNING: this channel layout is UNVALIDATED against any RealFlight coaxial model - verify the QGC Actuators tab and expect to retune.
ScaleComment: The "scale" column is the ONE thing here that is not free configuration, because it is a signal-domain conversion rather than a preference. PWMSim emits non-reversible Motor functions as [0,1] and EVERYTHING else as [-1,1] (PWMSim.cpp:77-88), while RealFlight wants [0,1] on every channel. So "unipolar" (pass through) is correct if and only if you assigned a Motor to that PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N>, and "bipolar" ((v+1)/2) is correct for servos, control surfaces and peripherals. Get it wrong and the failure is quiet: a motor read as bipolar idles at half throttle and never stops, and a surface read as unipolar loses its entire negative half and only ever deflects one way. RULE: change "scale" on a row whenever you change that channel's FUNC between a Motor and anything else. Here rf6 and rf7 are the two main rotors (Motor 1 and Motor 2, unipolar, disarm 0.0) and everything else is a servo (bipolar).
ReverseComment: There is deliberately no "reverse": true anywhere in this file. Direction is not a property of the pipe, it belongs to whoever configures the aircraft, and there are two places to set it - both outside this file. (1) PX4 side: the PWM_MAIN_REV parameter is a bitmask over output channels, bit N-1 for channel N, and it IS honoured in SITL - it is applied in MixingOutput::output_limit_calc_single (mixer_module.cpp:531) which runs upstream of PWMSim, so it reaches RealFlight. Reverse channel 7 with 'param set PWM_MAIN_REV 64'. (Note PWM_MAIN_MIN/MAX are a different story - PWMSim overwrites them with setAllMinValues/setAllMaxValues, so per-channel endpoint trimming does NOT work in SITL.) (2) RealFlight side: each servo has its own direction in the model's servo setup. ON A HELI, REVERSING A SINGLE SWASH SERVO IS ALMOST ALWAYS WRONG - the three swash outputs are a coordinated triple, and flipping one of them alone tilts the swash the wrong way about one axis while leaving the others, which reads as roll/pitch cross-coupling rather than a clean reversal. Fix swash direction in the RealFlight model's servo setup, or with CA_SP0_ANG*, not by reversing one channel. YAW ON A COAXIAL: if the heli yaws away from the commanded direction or spins up on liftoff, the fix is to swap the two rotor channels (rf6 <-> rf7) or reverse the model's rotor spin directions - NOT to reverse a swash servo.
DemixComment: HeliDemix IS DEFERRED OFF ON PURPOSE and is not in Options below. MIXING MUST LIVE ON EXACTLY ONE SIDE OF THE LINK. PX4's allocator is already the mixer: CA_AIRFRAME 12 with CA_SP0_ANG 300/60/180 turns roll/pitch into three swash servo positions, and this file ships those three positions to RealFlight UNTOUCHED - rf0/rf1/rf2 are literally Servo1/Servo2/Servo3 as the allocator computed them. So the RealFlight model must be a NON-MIXED, DIRECT-SERVO head: each RF channel wired straight through to the matching PWM channel, exactly the way a plane model is set up with ail/ele/thr/rud on ch1-4. If the model ALSO mixes, PX4's swash mix and the model's CCPM mix compose and roll and pitch cross-couple badly. That is the failure this default avoids. WHEN TO TURN IT BACK ON: only if your RealFlight coaxial model has a CCPM/eCCPM head whose mixing you cannot disable in the aircraft editor. Such a model wants roll/pitch/collective on rf0-2, not servo positions, so the bridge has to undo PX4's swash mix first - add "HeliDemix" back to Options (bitmask 9 becomes 13) and leave everything else alone. NOTE the demix inverts the SWASH triple only (rf0-2); it does not touch the rotor channels rf6/rf7, which carry rotor throttle either way. WHAT IT DOES WHEN ENABLED: converts the three swash servo outputs on rf0-2 back into the roll/pitch/collective triple RealFlight expects: roll=(s1-s2)/1.732, pitch=((s1+s2)/2-s3)/1.5, col=(s1+s2+s3)/3, recentered to 0..1. The two divisors are NOT cosmetic - they normalise the 0.866/0.75 gains the 120-degree head carries so RealFlight cyclic does not saturate early - and are exact only for the 300/60/180 geometry this airframe sets. See the single-rotor heli.json DemixComment for the full derivation. On a coaxial the 'collective' output of the demix is a swash artefact and is normally ignored by the model, since collective is made by the rotors; leave the demix off unless the model genuinely needs a mixed swash triple.
DisarmComment: rf0-2 each carry an explicit "disarm" of 0.5, the neutral swash position, matching the explicit "disarm" every motor row already states. With HeliDemix off by default these three rows are plain pass-through and nothing rewrites them after the fact, so hold-last would be safe here on that ground alone - but the values stay stated, because re-enabling HeliDemix for a CCPM model must not quietly reintroduce the hazard the single-rotor heli.json DisarmComment documents (a non-involutive in-place demix that railed a held, deflected swash within a few frames), and get_FAbridge_params.py rejects a hold-last row on any slot an option rewrites. Omitting disarm would default to -1 = "hold last output". The rotor rows rf6/rf7 carry disarm 0.0 so both rotors stop on disarm. The same disarm path also runs if any mapped control arrives NaN while armed.
YawComment: There is no dedicated yaw channel on a coaxial: yaw is the DIFFERENCE between the two rotor throttles rf6 (clockwise) and rf7 (counter-clockwise), which the allocator forms as rotor_CW = throttle - yaw and rotor_CCW = throttle + yaw. Both are Motors ([0,1], unipolar, disarm 0.0). This is why the two rotors must be independently drivable in the RealFlight model and must spin in OPPOSITE directions - tie them to one channel and PX4 loses all yaw authority. rf3-5 (the slots a single-rotor frame would use for a tail servo) are unassigned and idle at 0.5 here.
AuxComment: rf3-5 and rf8-11 (RF ch4-6 and ch9-12) are mapped but carry no function in the shipped airframe: PWM_MAIN_FUNC4/5/6 are explicitly 0 and FUNC9 and up are unset, so PX4 emits 0.0 on those outputs and the bipolar conversion turns that into a steady 0.5 - neutral, and byte-identical to the UnmappedDefault these channels used to fall back on. They are listed anyway so that using one takes a parameter change and nothing else: set PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N> to Landing_Gear, a governor, a Servo, a Peripheral via Actuator Set, or anything else PX4 offers, and it appears on RealFlight channel N with no edit to this file. Remember the scale rule above if what you assign is a Motor.
RfModel: informational only - aircraft is selected inside RealFlight

Channels.comment: swash plate servo 1        <- controls[0]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC1 = 201, sent raw (Servo1)
swash plate servo 2        <- controls[1]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC2 = 202, sent raw (Servo2)
swash plate servo 3        <- controls[2]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC3 = 203, sent raw (Servo3)
unassigned (no tail)       <- controls[3]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC4 = 0, set it to use
unassigned                 <- controls[4]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC5 = 0, set it to use
unassigned                 <- controls[5]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC6 = 0, set it to use
clockwise main rotor       <- controls[6]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC7 = 101 Motor 1
unassigned                 <- controls[8]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC9 to use
unassigned                 <- controls[9]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC10 to use
unassigned                 <- controls[10] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC11 to use
unassigned                 <- controls[11] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC12 to use
